content.problemStatement:
You are given a string SSS. Find the number of ways to choose an unordered pair of non-overlapping non-empty substrings of this string (let's denote them by s1s_1s1ŌĆŗ and s2s_2s2ŌĆŗ in such a way that s2s_2s2ŌĆŗ starts after s1s_1s1ŌĆŗ ends) such that their concatenation s1+s2s_1 + s_2s1ŌĆŗ+s2ŌĆŗ is a palindrome.

Two pairs (s1,s2)(s_1, s_2)(s1ŌĆŗ,s2ŌĆŗ) and (s1ŌĆ▓,s2ŌĆ▓)(s_1', s_2')(s1ŌĆ▓ŌĆŗ,s2ŌĆ▓ŌĆŗ) are different if s1s_1s1ŌĆŗ is chosen at a different position from s1ŌĆ▓s_1's1ŌĆ▓ŌĆŗ or s2s_2s2ŌĆŗ is chosen at a different position from s2ŌĆ▓s_2's2ŌĆ▓ŌĆŗ.
constraints: SSS contains only lowercase English letters
content.inputFormat: The first and only line of the input contains a single string SSS.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing one integer ŌĆö the number of ways to choose a valid pair of substrings.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
The following pairs of substrings can be chosen: ("a", "a"), ("a", "ba"), ("a", "bba"), ("ab", "a"), ("ab", "ba"), ("abb", "a"), ("b", "b").
